title: Total Response
summary: This page introduces the total response of a linear system as the sum of the zero-input response and the zero-state response. It gives a general expression for distinct roots, applies it to a series RLC example, and illustrates the decomposition with Figure 2.16.

figures.caption: Total response and its components; two response plots labeled (a) and (b) showing zero-state, zero-input, total, natural, and forced components.
